   "source": [
    "# Peak Detection: Basics\n",
    "\n",
    "[![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/tatsuki-washimi/gwexpy/blob/main/docs/web/en/user_guide/tutorials/advanced_peak_detection.ipynb)\n",
    "\n",
    "`gwexpy` provides the `find_peaks` method for easily detecting peaks in signals contained in `TimeSeries` or `FrequencySeries`. This internally wraps `scipy.signal.find_peaks`, but allows intuitive parameter specification using physical units (Hz, seconds, etc.).\n",
    "\n",
    "The `find_peaks` method returns the following two items:\n",
    "- **Peak Series**: A new Series object containing only the peak points\n",
    "- **Properties**: A dictionary of peak properties (such as prominence and width) returned by scipy"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "c6a81618",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T03:23:18.054343Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T03:23:18.054144Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T03:23:24.326765Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T03:23:24.326026Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 6.282075,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T03:23:24.329826+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T03:23:18.047751+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },

   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import warnings\n",
    "\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=UserWarning)\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=DeprecationWarning)\n",
    "\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "from astropy import units as u\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy import FrequencySeries, TimeSeries"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "6aa538db",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.001638,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T03:23:24.339214+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T03:23:24.337576+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 1. Peak Detection in FrequencySeries"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "cc16c84b",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T03:23:24.364976Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T03:23:24.364437Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T03:23:24.701846Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T03:23:24.699139Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.347726,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T03:23:24.703582+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T03:23:24.355856+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x500 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Create mock data\n",
    "df = 0.5 * u.Hz\n",
    "f_axis = np.arange(0, 500, df.value) * u.Hz\n",
    "data = np.exp(-f_axis.value / 100) + np.random.normal(0, 0.05, len(f_axis))\n",
    "\n",
    "# Add peaks\n",
    "peak_freqs_true = [50, 60, 120, 125, 300] * u.Hz\n",
    "for f in peak_freqs_true:\n",
    "    idx = int(f.value / df.value)\n",
    "    data[idx] += 1.0\n",
    "\n",
    "spec = FrequencySeries(data, df=df, unit=\"V/Hz\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# --- Basic detection (threshold only) ---\n",
    "# peaks is returned as a FrequencySeries object\n",
    "peaks, _ = spec.find_peaks(threshold=0.5)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))\n",
    "plt.plot(spec, label=\"Spectrum\", alpha=0.7)\n",
    "plt.plot(peaks, \"x\", color=\"red\", label=\"Basic Peaks\")\n",
    "plt.title(\"Basic Peak Detection\")\n",
    "plt.xlabel(\"Frequency [Hz]\")\n",
    "plt.ylabel(\"Amplitude [V/Hz]\")\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "8db74ec0",
   "metadata": {

     "duration": 0.006935,

   },
   "source": [
    "## 2. Constraints Using Physical Units (Distance & Width)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "47cbce60",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T03:23:24.749229Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T03:23:24.749009Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T03:23:25.012341Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T03:23:25.006345Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.277197,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T03:23:25.012997+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T03:23:24.735800+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [

     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x500 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Detected peaks: [ 50. 125. 300.] Hz\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "dist_constraint = 20 * u.Hz\n",
    "peaks_adv, props = spec.find_peaks(threshold=0.5, distance=dist_constraint)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))\n",
    "plt.plot(spec, label=\"Spectrum\", alpha=0.7)\n",
    "plt.plot(peaks_adv, \"o\", color=\"orange\", label=\"Distance Constraint (20Hz)\")\n",
    "plt.title(f\"Peak Detection with Distance Constraint ({dist_constraint})\")\n",
    "plt.xlabel(\"Frequency [Hz]\")\n",
    "plt.ylabel(\"Amplitude [V/Hz]\")\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Detected peaks: {peaks_adv.frequencies}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "0f60b1dc",
   "metadata": {

   },
   "source": [
    "## 3. Peak Detection in TimeSeries"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "fb3fcfcc",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-27T03:23:25.200227Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-27T03:23:25.199993Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-27T03:23:25.832107Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-27T03:23:25.831343Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.649048,
     "end_time": "2026-04-27T03:23:25.837230+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-27T03:23:25.188182+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x500 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "fs = 1024.0\n",
    "t = np.arange(0, 2.0, 1 / fs)\n",
    "ts_data = np.random.normal(0, 0.1, len(t))\n",
    "\n",
    "# Add pulses\n",
    "ts_data[int(0.5 * fs)] = 2.0\n",
    "ts_data[int(0.55 * fs)] = 1.8\n",
    "ts_data[int(1.2 * fs)] = 1.5\n",
    "\n",
    "ts = TimeSeries(ts_data, sample_rate=fs, unit=\"counts\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Ignore consecutive pulses within 0.1 seconds\n",
    "peaks_t, _ = ts.find_peaks(height=0.5, distance=0.1 * u.s)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))\n",
    "plt.plot(ts)\n",
    "plt.plot(peaks_t, \"v\", color=\"red\", label=\"Detected pulses (>100ms apart)\")\n",
    "plt.title(\"Pulse Detection in TimeSeries\")\n",
    "plt.xlabel(\"Time [s]\")\n",
    "plt.ylabel(\"Raw Counts\")\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  }
 ],
